workout.title: Chest Day - BODYBUILD
workout.description: Chest Day workout focusing on bodybuild training.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the low back.
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the low back.
Grip the bar with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width, wrists stacked over elbows and thumbs wrapped around the bar.
Retract and depress the shoulder blades (pinch the shoulder blades together) to create a stable chest platform.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar over the chest so it is balanced and steady.
Inhale, lower the bar under control to the mid-chest (sternum) keeping elbows at about a 45-degree angle from the torso.
Pause briefly with the bar touching or just above the chest while maintaining tightness through the core and legs.
Exhale and drive the bar upward by extending the elbows, pressing the bar back to the start position while keeping wrists neutral.
Avoid excessive elbow flare or bouncing the bar off the chest; maintain a controlled tempo and keep feet planted.
After the set, carefully re-rack the bar by guiding it back onto the bench supports before releasing your grip.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a supine pressing exercise performed on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also working the triceps and anterior delts. The lifter lowers a barbell to the chest and presses it back to full arm extension under control.

exercises.name: Smith Incline Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench inside the Smith machine at a 30–45° angle and load the bar with appropriate weight, securing plates with collars.
instructions: Set an incline bench inside the Smith machine at a 30–45° angle and load the bar with appropriate weight, securing plates with collars.
Sit on the bench and lie back so your head, upper back, and buttocks are fully supported (supine on an incline bench); plant feet flat on the floor for stability.
Position yourself so the bar aligns with the upper chest / clavicle area at the bottom of the movement; retract and squeeze your shoulder blades lightly to create a stable pressing platform.
Grip the bar slightly wider than shoulder-width with palms facing away; keep wrists neutral and elbows angled about 30–45° from the torso.
Unrack the bar by rotating the bar hooks or lifting slightly and sliding it out of the safety catches, keeping the motion controlled.
Inhale and lower the bar in a slow, controlled motion to the upper chest (just below the clavicle), keeping the elbows under the bar and the forearms vertical.
Pause briefly at the bottom without bouncing, then exhale and press the bar upward and slightly back toward the rack until arms are nearly extended (do not fully lock the elbows).
Maintain a tight core and do not over-arch the lower back; keep the head and upper back in contact with the bench throughout the set.
After the final repetition, carefully return the bar to the safety hooks by rotating it back into the rack or guiding it onto the stops.
Use safety stops or a spotter as needed and choose a controlled tempo to prioritize form and avoid shoulder strain.
exercises.description: Incline Smith bench press performed on an incline bench in a Smith machine to emphasize the upper portion of the pectorals; the fixed bar path provides stability while pressing from the upper chest toward lockout. Great for building upper chest mass with controlled, guided movement.

exercises.name: Barbell Close Grip Larsen Press
exercises.notes: Set up on a flat bench with a barbell racked at appropriate height; lie supine with your legs extended straight out on the bench (Larsen position) rather than feet on the floor.
instructions: Set up on a flat bench with a barbell racked at appropriate height; lie supine with your legs extended straight out on the bench (Larsen position) rather than feet on the floor.
Grip the bar with a close/narrow grip (hands about shoulder-width or slightly narrower), retract your shoulder blades, and unrack the bar to hold it over your chest with arms extended.
Lower the bar under control to your chest, keeping elbows tucked closer to your body, and allow the bar to briefly rest on your chest (dead stop).
Press the bar back up by driving through your chest and triceps until arms are fully extended; maintain core tension throughout since you have no leg drive.
Repeat for desired reps, then carefully re-rack the bar.
exercises.description: A close-grip variation of the bench press performed in the Larsen style with legs extended straight on the bench rather than planted on the floor. This eliminates leg drive and increases core stability demands while the narrow grip emphasizes triceps involvement alongside the pectorals and front delts.

exercises.name: Smith Decline Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set a decline bench inside the Smith machine and adjust safety stops so the bar cannot travel below your chest; load appropriate weight and hook your feet under the decline foot pads.
instructions: Set a decline bench inside the Smith machine and adjust safety stops so the bar cannot travel below your chest; load appropriate weight and hook your feet under the decline foot pads.
Lie supine on the decline bench with your head lower than your hips and feet secured; position the bar directly over your lower chest/upper sternum when arms are extended.
Grip the bar with a pronated (palms away) grip slightly wider than shoulder-width, wrists stacked over elbows and shoulder blades slightly retracted.
Unrack the bar by rotating the safety pins/handles and/or lifting straight up within the Smith's vertical path; take a full inhale.
Slowly lower the bar under control to the lower chest/upper sternum, keeping elbows at roughly a 45° angle from the torso to protect the shoulders.
Pause briefly at the bottom without bouncing, then exhale and press the bar upward until arms are nearly extended (do not lock elbows aggressively).
Repeat for the desired reps with a controlled tempo, then rotate the bar back into the safety catches before releasing your grip and sitting up.
exercises.description: Decline bench press performed on a Smith machine targets the lower portion of the pectorals by pressing a fixed bar while lying on a decline bench. The fixed vertical path stabilizes the bar, allowing focused pressing with assistance from the triceps and delts.

exercises.name: Barbell Reverse Grip Bench Press on Floor
exercises.notes: Lie flat on the floor with legs extended and grasp the barbell with a reverse (supinated/underhand) grip at shoulder-width.
instructions: Lie flat on the floor with legs extended and grasp the barbell with a reverse (supinated/underhand) grip at shoulder-width.
Unrack or lift the bar to position it directly over your chest with arms fully extended.
Lower the bar slowly until your upper arms touch or nearly touch the floor, keeping elbows tucked at about 45 degrees.
Press the bar back up to full arm extension, squeezing the chest at the top.
Maintain a flat back against the floor throughout the movement and control the tempo on each rep.
exercises.description: A floor-based bench press performed with a reverse (underhand/supinated) grip that emphasizes the upper portion of the pectorals while limiting range of motion. Lying on the floor, you lower the bar to the chest/upper torso and press it back up under control.

exercises.name: Barbell Decline Pullover
exercises.notes: Set a decline bench to a moderate angle and secure your feet under the pads so your body is stable and supine on the bench.
instructions: Set a decline bench to a moderate angle and secure your feet under the pads so your body is stable and supine on the bench.
Grasp a barbell with a pronated (overhand) grip about shoulder-width apart and lift it straight over your chest with elbows slightly bent.
Brace your core and keep your hips down to maintain a neutral spine throughout the movement.
Inhale and slowly lower the barbell in a controlled arc behind your head by hinging at the shoulders; keep a slight bend in the elbows and do not let them flare excessively.
Lower until you feel a deep stretch in the chest/lats or until the bar is roughly level with the crown of your head — do not go so far that your lower back overarches.
Exhale and pull the barbell back in the same arc to the start position over your chest by contracting the pectorals and lats; keep the movement smooth and controlled.
Repeat for the desired reps, maintaining steady tempo and full control on both lowering and lifting phases.
Use a spotter or lighter weight if you cannot control the bar safely behind your head; stop if you feel sharp shoulder or back pain.
exercises.description: Lying supine on a decline bench, the barbell pullover moves the weight in an arc from over the chest to behind the head, emphasizing a deep stretch and contraction of the chest while also engaging the lats and triceps.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.notes: Light weight, focus on form and range of motion

warmup.notes: Brief warmup to activate target muscles. Can skip if first working set feels warm.

workout.title: Back Day - BODYBUILD
workout.description: Back Day workout focusing on bodybuild training.

exercises.name: Barbell Reverse Grip Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Bend your knees slightly and hinge at the hips until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor (or at a comfortable 30–45° incline), keeping a neutral spine and head aligned with your spine.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and the barbell on the floor in front of you.
Bend your knees slightly and hinge at the hips until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor (or at a comfortable 30–45° incline), keeping a neutral spine and head aligned with your spine.
Reach down and grasp the barbell with an underhand (palms facing up) grip about shoulder-width apart; tighten your core and retract your shoulder blades slightly.
Initiate the movement by driving the elbows back and pulling the barbell toward your lower chest/upper abdomen, keeping elbows close to your sides to emphasize the lats.
Pause briefly at the top with the shoulder blades squeezed together and the chest slightly elevated, avoiding excessive spine extension or kipping.
Slowly lower the barbell back to the starting hang under control until arms are nearly extended, maintaining the hip hinge and neutral spine throughout.
Breathe out as you pull the bar up and inhale as you lower it; keep knees slightly bent and avoid rounding the lower back during the set.
exercises.description: A bent-over barbell row using an underhand (reverse) grip that emphasizes the lats while also recruiting the biceps and upper back musculature. Performed from a hinged, kneebent standing position and lifting the bar toward the lower chest/upper abdomen.

exercises.name: Lever Pullover (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Step 1: Sit on the machine with your back pressed firmly against the backrest, feet flat on the floor and knees bent at about 90 degrees.
instructions: Step 1: Sit on the machine with your back pressed firmly against the backrest, feet flat on the floor and knees bent at about 90 degrees.
Step 2: Adjust the seat and pivot so the lever handles start just above and behind your head when your arms are extended; select an appropriate weight.
Step 3: Reach up and grasp the handles with a comfortable (typically neutral or slightly pronated) grip, keeping a small, fixed bend in the elbows.
Step 4: Begin with the shoulders stretched overhead and the chest lifted slightly, maintaining a stable torso against the pad.
Step 5: Exhale and pull the lever in a controlled arc down and back toward your hips/upper thighs, leading with the elbows and focusing on squeezing the lats.
Step 6: Pause briefly at the bottom of the movement with elbows by your sides and a full lat contraction; avoid shrugging the shoulders.
Step 7: Inhale and slowly return the lever to the start position, resisting the weight and keeping tension on the lats without letting the shoulders fully relax.
Step 8: Repeat for desired reps; maintain strict form—no torso rocking, keep core engaged, and avoid locking the elbows at the end ranges.
exercises.description: Seated plate-loaded lever pullover that moves the arms in an overhead-to-hip arc to lengthen and contract the lats while also engaging the pectorals and serratus anterior. Performed on a lever machine with the torso supported by a backrest for stability.

exercises.name: Barbell Bent Over Wide Grip Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor; keep your spine neutral and head in line with the spine.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip-width apart and place a loaded barbell on the floor in front of you.
Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor; keep your spine neutral and head in line with the spine.
Grip the bar with a wide overhand grip (wider than shoulder-width) and let your arms hang fully extended.
Brace your core, inhale, then pull the bar toward your lower chest/upper abdomen by driving your elbows out and back, squeezing your shoulder blades together at the top.
Pause briefly at the top with the shoulders retracted, then exhale and lower the bar in a controlled manner until arms are extended.
Repeat for the desired repetitions while avoiding torso rotation, excessive momentum, or spinal rounding; keep knees slightly bent and maintain the hip hinge throughout.
exercises.description: A bent-over barbell row performed with a wide overhand grip that emphasizes the upper back by retracting the shoulder blades and pulling the bar toward the lower chest/upper abdomen. The torso stays hinged forward while the hips and spine stabilize the movement.

exercises.name: Lever Pronated Grip Seated Scapula Retraction Shrug (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height and load the machine so you can reach the handles with your arms extended and feet flat on the footrests; sit upright with a neutral spine and knees bent.
instructions: Adjust the seat height and load the machine so you can reach the handles with your arms extended and feet flat on the footrests; sit upright with a neutral spine and knees bent.
Grasp the handles with a pronated (overhand) grip and let your shoulders relax down and slightly forward to set the start position.
Initiate the movement by pulling the handles back using your shoulder blades: actively retract (pinch) the scapulae together while keeping the torso stable and chest neutral.
As you retract the scapulae, elevate your shoulders into a controlled shrug (shoulder elevation) and squeeze your traps and upper back at the top for 1–2 seconds.
Slowly reverse the motion: lower the shoulders and allow the scapulae to protract back to the start position under control — do not let the weight slam forward.
Keep the arms relatively straight with a slight bend at the elbows, avoid using momentum or torso swing, and breathe out during the contraction and in during the return.
exercises.description: Seated, pronated-grip plate-loaded exercise that emphasizes scapular retraction and an upward shrug to target the trapezius and upper back. Performed from a seated position using the machine handles to actively pinch the shoulder blades together and elevate the shoulders.

exercises.name: Barbell Behind The Back Shrug
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart, holding a barbell in front of your thighs with an overhand grip slightly wider than shoulder-width, arms fully extended.
instructions: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart, holding a barbell in front of your thighs with an overhand grip slightly wider than shoulder-width, arms fully extended.
Keep your chest up, core braced, and maintain a slight bend in the knees with a neutral spine throughout the movement.
Without bending your elbows, shrug your shoulders straight up toward your ears by contracting the traps; avoid rolling the shoulders forward or backward.
Hold the top position for 1-2 seconds, squeezing the traps hard at peak contraction.
Lower the bar slowly and under control back to the starting position, allowing the traps to fully stretch before the next rep.
exercises.description: Standing barbell shrug with the bar held in front of the thighs; raise the shoulders straight up toward the ears to target the upper trapezius, pause at the top, then lower back down under control. This is a fundamental trap-building exercise that emphasizes scapular elevation.

exercises.name: Barbell Seated Shrug
exercises.notes: Sit upright on the end of a flat bench with feet flat on the floor, holding a barbell with an overhand grip at shoulder-width, arms fully extended in front of your thighs.
instructions: Sit upright on the end of a flat bench with feet flat on the floor, holding a barbell with an overhand grip at shoulder-width, arms fully extended in front of your thighs.
Keeping your torso upright and core braced, shrug your shoulders straight up toward your ears without bending the elbows or rolling the shoulders.
Hold the top position for 1-2 seconds, squeezing the trapezius muscles at peak contraction.
Lower the barbell slowly and under control, allowing a full stretch of the traps at the bottom before the next rep.
Exhale as you shrug up and inhale as you lower; maintain strict form throughout all repetitions.
exercises.description: A seated barbell shrug isolates the trapezius by elevating the shoulders while holding a barbell; performing it seated reduces leg drive so the traps take most of the load. It emphasizes strong, vertical shoulder elevation (scapular elevation) with controlled lowering.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

workout.title: Shoulder Day - BODYBUILD
workout.description: Shoulder Day workout focusing on bodybuild training.

exercises.name: Barbell Clean and Press
exercises.notes: Drive through your heels, explosively extending hips, knees, and ankles (triple extension) while shrugging shoulders and pulling elbows high.
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart, barbell over mid-foot. Grip the bar just outside your knees with an overhand grip, chest up and core braced.
Drive through your heels, explosively extending hips, knees, and ankles (triple extension) while shrugging shoulders and pulling elbows high.
Quickly drop under the bar and rotate elbows forward to catch the barbell in a front-rack position on your shoulders, absorbing with a partial squat.
Stand tall from the front-rack position, then press the bar overhead by extending your arms until elbows are fully locked out.
Lower the bar back to the front-rack position, then return it to the floor with control by hinging at the hips and bending the knees.
exercises.description: A compound Olympic-style lift combining a barbell clean (lifting the bar to a front-rack position) followed immediately by a press overhead, training the shoulders along with legs, hips and upper back. It develops explosive power, pressing strength, and full-body coordination.

exercises.name: Landmine Shoulders Press with Viking Press Attachment
exercises.notes: Stand facing the landmine with feet shoulder-width apart and a slight bend in the knees; grip the Viking press handles so the attachment sits at shoulder height with elbows roughly tucked under the wrists.
instructions: Stand facing the landmine with feet shoulder-width apart and a slight bend in the knees; grip the Viking press handles so the attachment sits at shoulder height with elbows roughly tucked under the wrists.
Brace your core, keep your chest up and neutral spine, and position the bar angled away from you so it can travel upward without hitting your head.
Inhale, then press the handles upward and slightly forward in a controlled arc until your arms are fully extended overhead or slightly behind the head, avoiding excessive arching of the lower back.
Pause briefly at the top with shoulders engaged and elbows unlocked but not hyperextended.
Exhale as you slowly lower the handles back down to the starting shoulder-height position under control, maintaining tension in the delts and core.
Repeat for the desired reps; keep movement smooth and controlled, avoid leaning the torso excessively back or using lower-body drive to move the load.
Adjust stance or hand position to shift emphasis (wider grip = more lateral delt emphasis; closer grip = more anterior delt/pectorals) and lower the weight if you cannot maintain strict form.
exercises.description: A standing landmine press using a Viking press attachment that emphasizes overhead pressing from the shoulders; the movement drives the handles upward from shoulder height to full arm extension, targeting the delts while the triceps and upper chest assist.

exercises.name: Barbell Rear Delt Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips and slightly bend the knees until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor (or at a 30–45° angle if mobility requires), keeping a neutral spine and chest up.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and a barbell on the floor in front of you.
Hinge at the hips and slightly bend the knees until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor (or at a 30–45° angle if mobility requires), keeping a neutral spine and chest up.
Grip the barbell with an overhand (pronated) grip slightly wider than shoulder width so elbows can travel out to the sides.
Brace your core and retract your shoulder blades, maintaining a stable torso throughout the movement.
Initiate the rep by driving the elbows outward and up, pulling the bar toward the upper chest/upper abdomen while keeping the elbows high to emphasize the rear delts.
At the top, pause briefly and squeeze the rear delts and upper back without shrugging the shoulders.
Slowly lower the bar under control back to the start position, fully extending the arms while maintaining the hip hinge and neutral spine.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions; avoid using momentum or excessive torso rotation to lift the weight.
exercises.description: A bent-over barbell row variation that specifically emphasizes the rear delts by rowing with elbows flared out to the sides. Performed from a hip-hinged standing position to target the posterior shoulder and upper back.

exercises.name: Lever Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height so the handles sit at your sides roughly at hip level when seated; select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Adjust the seat height so the handles sit at your sides roughly at hip level when seated; select an appropriate weight.
Sit upright with your back against the pad, feet flat on the floor, and grasp the handles with a neutral grip (palms facing inward).
Keep a slight bend in the elbows and the forearms relatively fixed; start with the arms hanging at your sides.
Exhale and raise your upper arms out to the sides in a smooth arc until your elbows reach shoulder height (arms roughly parallel to the floor).
Keep your torso still and avoid leaning or using momentum; do not excessively shrug the shoulders.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining tension in the delts.
Inhale and slowly lower the handles back to the start position with controlled movement.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions while maintaining form throughout.
exercises.description: Seated machine lever lateral raise isolates the lateral delts by abducting the arms out to the sides against resistance, emphasizing controlled movement and minimal torso motion. It targets shoulder width and middle deltoid development.

exercises.name: Lever Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Set the machine: choose an appropriate weight, adjust the seat height so the pivot of the lever lines up with your shoulder joint and the handles start near your hips.
instructions: Set the machine: choose an appropriate weight, adjust the seat height so the pivot of the lever lines up with your shoulder joint and the handles start near your hips.
Sit upright with your back pressed into the seat pad, feet flat on the floor, and chest up; grasp the handles with a neutral or slightly pronated grip.
Start with your elbows slightly bent and arms resting at your sides (not locked), maintaining a neutral wrist and steady grip.
Exhale and lift the handles out to your sides in a controlled arc, leading with the elbow so the upper arm reaches about parallel to the floor (shoulder height).
Keep your torso still and avoid shrugging your shoulders; use your delts to move the weight rather than momentum or back extension.
Pause briefly at the top with the elbows slightly higher than the wrists, feeling tension in the delts and traps for scapular stability.
Inhale and slowly lower the handles back to the start position with control, maintaining the slight elbow bend.
Repeat for the desired number of reps. Prioritize controlled tempo, full range without swinging, and maintain scapular stability throughout.
exercises.description: Seated lever lateral raise is a machine exercise that isolates the lateral head of the delts by abducting the arms out to the sides from a seated, supported position. It emphasizes controlled movement to raise the arms to about shoulder height while minimizing trunk movement.

exercises.name: Barbell Rear Delt Raise
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart, holding a barbell behind your body with an overhand grip slightly wider than shoulder-width.
instructions: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart, holding a barbell behind your body with an overhand grip slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Keep your chest up, shoulders back, and maintain a slight bend in the elbows throughout the movement.
Exhale and lift the bar by driving your elbows out to the sides and slightly back, squeezing the rear delts at the top.
Raise until your upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor or slightly below shoulder height, pause briefly.
Inhale and slowly lower the bar back to the starting position under control, avoiding momentum.
exercises.description: Standing upright rear delt raise using a barbell held behind the body. The bar is lifted by driving the elbows out and back to target the posterior deltoids and upper back while maintaining an upright torso position.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

workout.title: Arm Day - BODYBUILD
workout.description: Arm Day workout focusing on bodybuild training.

exercises.name: Barbell Close Grip Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set up a flat bench in a rack or on a bench press station and load the barbell safely; position the bar at arm level for easy unracking.
instructions: Set up a flat bench in a rack or on a bench press station and load the barbell safely; position the bar at arm level for easy unracking.
Lie supine (on your back) on the bench with feet flat on the floor, knees bent, shoulders retracted and pressed into the bench for stability.
Grip the barbell with a close grip: hands about shoulder-width apart (index fingers roughly in line with the shoulders) or slightly narrower; keep wrists neutral and stacked over elbows.
Unrack the bar by straightening your arms and moving the bar directly above your sternum; keep a tight core and stable foot drive.
Inhale and lower the bar under control to the lower chest / sternum area, keeping elbows tucked closer to your body (about 45 degrees or less from the torso) to emphasize the triceps.
Pause briefly with the bar lightly touching the chest (do not bounce), then exhale and press the bar upward by extending the elbows until arms are nearly straight but not hyperextended.
Maintain wrist alignment and shoulder position throughout; avoid flaring the elbows wide and keep the movement vertical over the chest.
Rerack the bar with control after the set; use a spotter when lifting heavy or if you are near failure.
Progress by increasing load gradually, or modify grip width slightly to change emphasis; stop or reduce range if you feel joint pain (especially in wrists or elbows).
exercises.description: A compound supine pressing exercise performed with a narrow (close) grip to place primary emphasis on the triceps while still recruiting the pectorals and anterior delts. Performed on a flat bench, the bar is lowered to the chest and pressed back to full elbow extension.

exercises.name: Barbell Seated Overhead Triceps Extension
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor, knees bent, and spine neutral; brace your abs.
instructions: Sit upright on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor, knees bent, and spine neutral; brace your abs.
Grasp the barbell with a shoulder-width (or slightly narrower) overhand grip and press it straight overhead until your arms are fully extended; this is the start position.
Keep your upper arms (elbows) pointing forward and close to your head, maintaining a stable shoulder position.

Inhale and slowly lower the barbell behind your head by bending only at the elbows, keeping the upper arms stationary until your forearms are about parallel to the floor or you feel a stretch in the triceps.
Pause briefly, then exhale and press the barbell back up by extending the elbows to return to the fully extended overhead position while keeping the core braced.
Avoid excessive lower-back arching or letting the elbows flare out; if necessary use lighter weight or a spotter to maintain safe form.
After completing your set, have a spotter help lower the bar to your chest/thighs before removing it from your lap, or carefully rack the bar with assistance.
exercises.description: Seated barbell overhead triceps extension is a single-joint exercise that targets the triceps by extending the elbows from an overhead position while the upper arms remain stationary. It emphasizes the long head of the triceps and requires core and shoulder stability.

exercises.name: Barbell Preacher Curl
exercises.notes: Set up a preacher bench and load a straight or EZ bar with an appropriate weight; sit so your chest contacts the pad and your armpits rest at the top edge of the pad.
instructions: Set up a preacher bench and load a straight or EZ bar with an appropriate weight; sit so your chest contacts the pad and your armpits rest at the top edge of the pad.
Place feet flat on the floor and grasp the bar with a shoulder-width, underhand (supinated) grip so your upper arms lie flat against the pad and wrists are neutral.
Start with your arms extended and the bar held just above full extension (do not lock the elbows); take a full breath in to brace the torso.
Exhale and curl the bar upward by contracting the biceps, keeping the upper arms pressed against the pad and avoiding shoulder or torso movement.
Pause briefly at the top when you feel a strong biceps contraction (bar near chest/shoulder level), then inhale and slowly lower the bar back to the starting position with controlled tension.
Keep the movement strict—no swinging, jerking, or lifting the elbows off the pad—and stop short of joint hyperextension at the bottom.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, adjusting grip width to slightly vary biceps emphasis as needed.
exercises.description: Seated on a preacher (incline) bench with upper arms supported on the pad, curl a barbell upward by flexing the elbows to isolate the biceps, then lower under control to stretch the muscle.

exercises.name: Barbell Prone Incline Curl
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and place a barbell on the floor or a low rack within reach.
instructions: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and place a barbell on the floor or a low rack within reach.
Lie prone (face down) on the bench so your chest rests on the pad and your feet/braced on the floor for stability; allow your arms to hang straight down toward the bar.
Grasp the barbell with a shoulder-width, underhand (supinated) grip and keep your wrists neutral.
Keep your torso pressed into the bench and your upper arms fixed; avoid moving the shoulders or upper body during the lift.
Exhale and curl the barbell by flexing the elbows until your forearms approach your biceps, squeezing the biceps at the top of the movement.
Inhale and slowly lower the barbell under control back to full elbow extension so the arms hang straight.
Repeat for the desired reps, maintaining strict form and avoiding momentum or swinging to preserve isolation of the biceps.
exercises.description: Prone incline barbell curls are performed lying face-down on an inclined bench and curling a barbell upward, isolating the biceps by minimizing body swing and shoulder involvement. The incline position lengthens the biceps at the start and emphasizes the long head during the curl.

exercises.name: Barbell Reverse Close grip Bench Pres
exercises.notes: Set a flat bench in a rack at a height where you can unrack and rerack the bar safely while lying flat.
instructions: Set a flat bench in a rack at a height where you can unrack and rerack the bar safely while lying flat.
Lie supine on the bench with feet flat on the floor, knees bent, and a small natural arch in the low back; eyes under the bar.
Grasp the bar with a close, reverse (supinated) grip — palms facing your head — hands about shoulder-width or slightly narrower; wrap thumbs around the bar for safety.
Unrack the bar with the help of a spotter if needed and hold it straight over your chest with elbows locked and forearms vertical.
Inhale and slowly lower the bar under control to the upper chest/clavicular area (just below the collarbone), keeping elbows tucked slightly toward your sides and wrists stacked over forearms.
Pause briefly at the chest without bouncing, then exhale and press the bar back up along the same vertical path until arms are nearly locked out.
Repeat for the desired repetitions, maintaining tight shoulders and a neutral wrist; use a spotter and lighter loads until comfortable with the grip and bar path.
After the set, carefully rack the bar with control and the assistance of a spotter if necessary.
exercises.description: A supine barbell press performed with a close, reverse (supinated) grip that emphasizes triceps activation while also engaging the chest and front deltoids. The bar is lowered to the mid-chest area and pressed back to full arm extension with elbows kept closer to the body.

exercises.name: Barbell Standing Wrist Curl
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart, knees soft and core braced.
instructions: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart, knees soft and core braced.
Grasp a barbell with an underhand (palms up) shoulder-width grip and let it hang at thigh level with your arms fully extended and elbows stationary at your sides.
Keep your torso still and shoulders relaxed; avoid bending the elbows or using body swing.
Curl your wrists upward as far as possible, lifting the barbell by flexing the wrists and squeezing the forearms at the top.
Pause briefly at the top for a controlled contraction.
Slowly lower the barbell by extending the wrists until you feel a stretch in the forearms, returning to the start position.
Repeat for desired repetitions while maintaining strict form and preventing momentum from the shoulders or torso.
exercises.description: A standing isolation exercise that works the wrist flexors by flexing the wrists while holding a barbell with an underhand grip. Performed upright, it emphasizes forearm strength and grip endurance.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Lever Belt Sumo Squat
exercises.notes: Stand on the platform facing the machine with the lever belt secured low on your hips; grab the front handles for light support if available.
instructions: Stand on the platform facing the machine with the lever belt secured low on your hips; grab the front handles for light support if available.
Position your feet much wider than shoulder-width with toes pointed out at ~30–45 degrees (sumo stance).
Brace your core, keep chest tall and a neutral spine, and allow a slight forward torso lean while maintaining an upright upper body.

Begin the movement by pushing your hips back and bending at the knees, lowering yourself into a squat until your thighs are roughly parallel to the platform or a comfortable depth where knees track over toes.
Keep your knees tracking in line with your toes and distribute weight through your heels and mid-foot; avoid collapsing the knees inward.
Drive forcefully through the heels and hips to return to standing, fully extending the hips at the top while resisting the lever’s load throughout the motion.
Control the descent on each rep, breathe in on the way down and exhale as you drive up, and perform repetitions with a steady tempo.
exercises.description: A wide-stance (sumo) squat performed with a lever belt/harness attached to a machine; emphasizes hip drive and inner-thigh engagement while reducing load on the spine. Use a wide, turned-out foot position and sit back between the heels, then drive up through the hips.

exercises.name: Barbell Clean Deadlift
exercises.notes: Bend at the hips and knees to grip the bar with a double overhand (or mixed) grip slightly outside the knees; hips should be lower than a conventional deadlift but higher than a full squat, torso more upright and chest up.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart with the bar over the mid-foot and toes pointing slightly outward; approach the bar from a standing position (not kneeling).

Bend at the hips and knees to grip the bar with a double overhand (or mixed) grip slightly outside the knees; hips should be lower than a conventional deadlift but higher than a full squat, torso more upright and chest up.
Set a neutral spine and pull the shoulders slightly down and back to engage the upper back and lats; keep the head in a neutral position (eyes forward).
Take a full breath and brace the core; push the knees out so they track over the toes and keep the bar close to the shins.

Drive through the heels/mid-foot by extending the knees and hips simultaneously in a powerful, vertical movement — focus on knee extension to emphasize the quads while finishing with hip extension to engage glutes and hamstrings.
Keep the bar close to the body, shrug slightly at the top to engage the traps, and stand fully tall without hyperextending the lower back.
Lower the bar under control by pushing the hips back and bending the knees, maintaining a neutral spine; return the bar to the floor with a controlled descent and reset for the next rep.
Avoid rounding the back, letting the bar drift away from the body, or initiating the lift with the hips alone; maintain a coordinated knee and hip drive throughout.
exercises.description: The barbell clean deadlift is a deadlift variation that starts from a lower, more upright position similar to the clean, placing primary emphasis on the quadriceps while also recruiting the posterior chain and upper back. It trains a strong, vertical bar path and powerful knee and hip extension.

exercises.name: Lever Romanian Deadlift (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Load the lever machine with appropriate plates and unlock the lever if necessary; stand on the foot platform facing forward with feet about hip-width apart.
instructions: Load the lever machine with appropriate plates and unlock the lever if necessary; stand on the foot platform facing forward with feet about hip-width apart.
Reach down and grasp the machine handles with a neutral, overhand grip; set a small bend in the knees and lift the chest so your spine is neutral (standing position).
Inhale, brace your core, and initiate the movement by pushing your hips backward (hip hinge) while keeping the bar/lever close to your legs and maintaining a straight neutral spine.

Lower until you feel a strong but controlled stretch in the hamstrings (typically to mid-shin or when torso is near parallel to the floor), keeping the knees soft and not letting them travel forward excessively.

Pause briefly at the bottom while maintaining tension in the hamstrings and a neutral neck position (look slightly forward), then exhale and drive the hips forward to return to the upright start position by contracting the hamstrings and squeezing the glutes.
Keep the movement slow and controlled on the descent (eccentric) and avoid rounding the lower back; repeat for the desired number of reps and re-rack/lock the lever when finished.
exercises.description: A hip-hinge movement performed on a plate-loaded lever machine that emphasizes the hamstrings while loading the posterior chain. Stand on the platform and hinge at the hips with a slight knee bend, lowering the lever while keeping a neutral spine and the load close to the body.

exercises.name: Lever Belt Romanian Deadlift
exercises.notes: Set the lever machine to the desired load and securely fasten the belt around your hips so the lever is attached and aligned with your center of mass.
instructions: Set the lever machine to the desired load and securely fasten the belt around your hips so the lever is attached and aligned with your center of mass.
Stand on the platform with feet about hip-width apart, toes pointing forward, and a slight bend in the knees; keep the chest up and shoulders back (neutral spine).
Brace your core and take a full breath, then begin the movement by pushing your hips straight back (hip hinge), allowing the torso to lower while the knees stay softly bent.
Lower until you feel a firm stretch in the hamstrings or your torso is roughly parallel to the floor — do not round the lower back.
Drive your hips forward by contracting the hamstrings and glutes to return to standing, keeping the spine neutral and avoiding hip thrust hyperextension at the top.
Exhale as you stand, control the descent on the next rep, and maintain tension through the forearms and core throughout each repetition.
exercises.description: A hinge-dominant deadlift variation performed with a belt attached to a lever machine that emphasizes eccentric loading of the hamstrings and hip extension. The movement uses a controlled hip hinge with a slight knee bend while the belt/load pulls you forward.

exercises.name: Smith One Leg Floor Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Set the Smith machine bar to shoulder height and load an appropriate weight; step under the bar and position it across your upper back/traps.
instructions: Set the Smith machine bar to shoulder height and load an appropriate weight; step under the bar and position it across your upper back/traps.
Grip the bar with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width, brace your core and unrack the bar by standing up straight.
Place the working foot flat on the floor under your hips; position the non-working foot crossed behind or with the toe lightly touching the floor for balance (standing position).
Keep the torso upright, knees slightly soft (not locked) on the working leg to avoid hyperextension.
Exhale and raise your heel on the working leg as high as possible by contracting the calf (plantarflexion), squeezing at the top.
Inhale and slowly lower the heel back down under control until the ankle is dorsiflexed to the starting position (feel a stretch in the calf).
Perform the desired number of repetitions on one leg, then carefully rack the bar and switch legs to repeat on the other side.
exercises.description: A single-leg standing calf raise performed with a Smith machine bar across the upper back to isolate the calf of the working leg while the other foot is lightly placed for balance. Raise the heel by plantarflexing the ankle and lower under control for a full range of motion.

exercises.name: Lever Rotary Calf
exercises.notes: Grip the side handles for stability and ensure the cable attachment is secured around the balls of your feet.
instructions: Sit on the machine with your back against the pad and legs extended straight out in front of you; place the balls of your feet against the footplate with heels free to move.
Grip the side handles for stability and ensure the cable attachment is secured around the balls of your feet.
Start with ankles in a neutral or slightly dorsiflexed position to feel a stretch in the calves.
Press through the balls of your feet to plantarflex the ankles, pointing your toes away from you and squeezing the calves at peak contraction.
Slowly return to the starting position with control, allowing a full stretch in the calves before the next rep.
exercises.description: Lever rotary calf is a machine-based calf raise performed from a seated position with legs extended, isolating plantarflexion to target the calf muscles through a controlled range of motion using a cable-pulley system.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Squat
exercises.notes: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height.
instructions: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height. Position yourself under the bar so it rests across your upper traps (high-bar) or rear delts (low-bar) depending on preference.
Grip the bar with hands just outside shoulder width, elbows pointing down and chest up. Stand with feet roughly shoulder-width apart and toes pointed slightly outward.
Brace your core and take a full breath in, then unrack the bar by extending your legs and taking 1–2 controlled steps backward to clear the rack; establish a stable, shoulder-width stance.
Keep a neutral spine and an upright chest. Initiate the movement by pushing your hips back slightly and bending at the knees, sending your knees outward to track over your toes.
Descend in a controlled manner until your thighs are at least parallel to the floor (or deeper if mobility allows) while keeping weight on your midfoot/heels and maintaining a braced core.
Avoid knee collapse (valgus) — consciously push knees outward and keep toes engaged. Do not let the lower back round; maintain a neutral spine throughout.
Drive through your heels and midfoot to stand back up, exhaling as you ascend and extending hips and knees simultaneously until fully upright.
Reset your breathing and bracing between reps as needed. When finished, step forward under control and re-rack the bar securely.
Use appropriate weight, a spotter or safety pins in the rack if lifting near maximum loads, and adjust stance or bar position to reduce discomfort in shoulders or lower back.
exercises.description: Barbell back squat is a compound, standing lower-body exercise that primarily develops the quadriceps while also engaging the glutes, hamstrings and core for stability. The barbell rests across the upper back while you descend by bending hips and knees, then drive back up to standing.

exercises.name: Smith Standing Leg Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Set the Smith bar to an appropriate height and load desired weight; stand facing forward so the bar rests across your upper back/traps or held just behind the hips with a firm grip.
instructions: Set the Smith bar to an appropriate height and load desired weight; stand facing forward so the bar rests across your upper back/traps or held just behind the hips with a firm grip.
Stand upright with feet hip-width apart (toes pointing forward) on a flat surface or on a small block/plate if extra range of motion is desired; keep knees nearly straight with a slight natural bend.
Unrack the bar and stabilize your core and posture; keep chest up and shoulders back throughout the movement.
Exhale and press through the balls of both feet to raise your heels as high as possible, producing a full contraction in the calves; pause briefly at the top.
Inhale and slowly lower your heels back down to the starting position with control; if using a block, allow the heels to drop slightly below the toes for added stretch.
Complete the desired number of repetitions with a controlled tempo, then carefully re-rack the bar ensuring it is secure before stepping away.
exercises.description: A loaded standing calf raise performed in a Smith machine. The lifter rises onto the balls of the feet to concentrically contract the calf muscles, then lowers with control to work the gastrocnemius/soleus complex.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Squat
exercises.notes: Setup: Place a flat bench behind you at a height that allows your thighs to reach roughly parallel to the floor when seated; position a loaded barbell across your upper back (traps), grip the bar just outside shoulder width and stand with feet about hip-to-shoulder width, toes slightly turned out.
instructions: Setup: Place a flat bench behind you at a height that allows your thighs to reach roughly parallel to the floor when seated; position a loaded barbell across your upper back (traps), grip the bar just outside shoulder width and stand with feet about hip-to-shoulder width, toes slightly turned out.
Brace your core, pull your shoulder blades back, and keep your chest up and eyes forward; this is a standing start position.
Begin the descent by sending your hips back slightly and bending at the knees, maintaining an upright torso and keeping knees tracking over your toes.
Lower under control until your glutes lightly touch the bench (do not relax fully or sit down), pause 1 second while maintaining tension in the legs and core.
Drive through your heels and midfoot, extend the knees and hips to stand back up to the starting position while exhaling; keep the bar stable on your upper back throughout.
Repeat for the desired reps. Use a spotter or safety pins if using heavy loads and avoid bouncing off the bench or rounding the lower back.
exercises.description: A back‑loaded squat variation where you lower yourself to lightly tap a bench behind you, emphasizing knee‑dominant loading to target the quadriceps while maintaining an upright torso. The bench enforces a consistent depth and helps with technique for beginners and lifters training tempo.

exercises.name: Lever Romanian Deadlift (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Load the lever machine with appropriate plates and unlock the lever if necessary; stand on the foot platform facing forward with feet about hip-width apart.
instructions: Load the lever machine with appropriate plates and unlock the lever if necessary; stand on the foot platform facing forward with feet about hip-width apart.
Reach down and grasp the machine handles with a neutral, overhand grip; set a small bend in the knees and lift the chest so your spine is neutral (standing position).
Inhale, brace your core, and initiate the movement by pushing your hips backward (hip hinge) while keeping the bar/lever close to your legs and maintaining a straight neutral spine.

Lower until you feel a strong but controlled stretch in the hamstrings (typically to mid-shin or when torso is near parallel to the floor), keeping the knees soft and not letting them travel forward excessively.

Pause briefly at the bottom while maintaining tension in the hamstrings and a neutral neck position (look slightly forward), then exhale and drive the hips forward to return to the upright start position by contracting the hamstrings and squeezing the glutes.
Keep the movement slow and controlled on the descent (eccentric) and avoid rounding the lower back; repeat for the desired number of reps and re-rack/lock the lever when finished.
exercises.description: A hip-hinge movement performed on a plate-loaded lever machine that emphasizes the hamstrings while loading the posterior chain. Stand on the platform and hinge at the hips with a slight knee bend, lowering the lever while keeping a neutral spine and the load close to the body.

exercises.name: Lever Sitting Toe Raise
exercises.notes: Place the balls of your feet on the footplate with your heels hanging off the edge and grasp the machine handles or sides of the bench for stability.
instructions: Sit on the machine bench with your knees bent at about 90 degrees and the padded lever resting across your lower thighs just above the knees.
Place the balls of your feet on the footplate with your heels hanging off the edge and grasp the machine handles or sides of the bench for stability.
Start with your heels lowered to feel a stretch in the calves (ankles dorsiflexed).
Exhale and press down through the balls of your feet, plantarflexing the ankles to raise your heels as high as possible while keeping the knees stationary.
Hold the top position for a brief pause, squeezing the calves, then slowly lower your heels back to the start position under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, keeping a controlled tempo and maintaining upright torso and stable knee position throughout.
exercises.description: A seated calf-raise performed on a lever-style machine that isolates the lower leg by plantarflexing the ankle against resistance while the knees are bent. It emphasizes the soleus and gastrocnemius to build calf strength and size.

exercises.name: Lever Standing Hip Extension
exercises.notes: Set the desired weight on the lever machine and position the lower-leg pad so it rests just above the ankle of the working leg.
instructions: Set the desired weight on the lever machine and position the lower-leg pad so it rests just above the ankle of the working leg.
Stand facing the machine and place your chest/forearms or hands on the support pad/handles for stability; plant the non-working foot flat on the floor and keep it slightly bent for balance.
Place the working foot against the lever pad behind you so the pad contacts the lower shin/ankle area, with the working knee either slightly bent or in a comfortable neutral angle.
Brace your core, keep your torso upright and hips square, and avoid overarching the lower back throughout the movement.
Exhale and drive the working leg straight back by extending the hip, squeezing the glutes at the top of the movement until the leg is in line with or slightly behind the torso.
Pause briefly at full extension while maintaining a tight core, then inhale and slowly return the lever to the starting position under control without letting the weight stack slam.
Perform the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other leg and repeat.
exercises.description: A machine-based standing hip extension where one leg pushes a rear lever backward to extend the hip, emphasizing the glute muscles with assistance from the hamstrings. Performed while braced against a pad/handle to isolate hip extension through a controlled rearward kick.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and the barbell over mid-foot; use an overhand (pronated) grip slightly wider than shoulder width.
Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
Hold the bar with arms extended and shoulders protracted, chest up and eyes looking a few feet in front of you to maintain a neutral neck.
Initiate the rep by driving the elbows back and up, pulling the bar toward your lower chest / upper abdomen while squeezing the shoulder blades together.
Keep the elbows close to the body (not flaring excessively) and avoid using momentum from the hips; breathe out as you pull.
Lower the bar slowly and under control to the starting position with arms extended while maintaining the hip hinge and neutral spine; breathe in on the descent.
Repeat for the desired reps, controlling tempo and keeping the torso stable — if you cannot maintain a flat back, reduce weight or stop and reset.
exercises.description: Standing hip-hinged barbell row where you pull a loaded barbell toward the lower chest/upper abdomen; primarily targets the lats and upper back while requiring posterior chain and core stabilization. Maintain a flat back and drive the elbows back to retract the shoulder blades.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the low back.
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the low back.
Grip the bar with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width, wrists stacked over elbows and thumbs wrapped around the bar.
Retract and depress the shoulder blades (pinch the shoulder blades together) to create a stable chest platform.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar over the chest so it is balanced and steady.
Inhale, lower the bar under control to the mid-chest (sternum) keeping elbows at about a 45-degree angle from the torso.
Pause briefly with the bar touching or just above the chest while maintaining tightness through the core and legs.
Exhale and drive the bar upward by extending the elbows, pressing the bar back to the start position while keeping wrists neutral.
Avoid excessive elbow flare or bouncing the bar off the chest; maintain a controlled tempo and keep feet planted.
After the set, carefully re-rack the bar by guiding it back onto the bench supports before releasing your grip.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a supine pressing exercise performed on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also working the triceps and anterior delts. The lifter lowers a barbell to the chest and presses it back to full arm extension under control.

exercises.name: Barbell Wide Grip Upright Row
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart, knees slightly bent and core braced (standing position).
instructions: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart, knees slightly bent and core braced (standing position).
Grip the barbell with an overhand (pronated) grip wider than shoulder-width and let the bar hang at thigh level.
Keep the chest up, shoulders back, and torso vertical; do not lean forward or excessively arch the lower back.
Initiate the movement by driving the elbows out and upward, keeping the bar close to the body.
Raise the bar to about upper-chest / collarbone height with elbows higher than the wrists; lead with the elbows, not the hands.
Pause briefly at the top with shoulder blades slightly squeezed, avoiding shrug-only motion or lifting the bar above shoulder level.
Lower the bar slowly and under control back to the starting position to complete one rep.
Keep wrists neutral (not excessively bent) and breathe out on the lift, inhale on the descent.
Use a moderate weight and controlled tempo to avoid shoulder impingement; stop if you feel anterior shoulder pain.
exercises.description: Standing wide-grip upright rows use an overhand, wider-than-shoulder grip to pull a barbell vertically toward the upper chest, emphasizing the lateral delts and upper traps while also engaging the upper back and grip muscles.

tips: Focus on explosive concentric movements.
Take full rest between sets for neural recovery.
Prioritize form over weight - quality reps build strength.

Keep a neutral spine and an upright chest. Initiate the movement by pushing your hips back slightly and bending at the knees, sending your knees outward to track over your toes.

exercises.description: Barbell back squat is a compound, standing lower-body exercise that primarily develops the quadriceps while also engaging the glutes, hamstrings and core for stability. exercises.name: Barbell Romanian Deadlift
exercises.notes: Stand upright holding a barbell with an overhand (or mixed) grip, feet about hip-width apart and the bar resting against your thighs; maintain a neutral spine and engaged core.
instructions: Stand upright holding a barbell with an overhand (or mixed) grip, feet about hip-width apart and the bar resting against your thighs; maintain a neutral spine and engaged core.
Set a small, consistent bend in the knees (soft knees) — do not bend into a squat; this is a hinge at the hips, not a knee-dominant lift.
Take a breath in, brace your core, then push your hips back while keeping the chest up and spine neutral; allow the bar to travel down close to the front of your legs.
Lower the bar until you feel a strong but safe stretch in the hamstrings (typically to mid‑shin), keeping the bar close to the body and shoulders pulled back — avoid rounding the lower back.
Pause briefly at the bottom while maintaining tension in the hamstrings and back; keep your head in a neutral alignment (looking a few feet in front of you).
Exhale and drive your hips forward by contracting the glutes and hamstrings to return to the standing start position; do not hyperextend the lower back at the top.
Control the descent and ascent on every rep, using a weight that lets you maintain a neutral spine and full hip hinge pattern.
If grip is limiting, switch to a mixed grip or use straps; always prioritize form over load and stop if you feel lumbar rounding or sharp pain.
exercises.description: A bilateral hip-hinge performed from standing that emphasizes the hamstrings by lowering a loaded barbell along the thighs with a neutral spine and slight knee bend; returned to standing by driving the hips forward and squeezing the glutes. Builds posterior chain strength and improves hip extension.

exercises.name: Lever Hip Thrust (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Set the machine to an appropriate weight and sit with your upper back firmly against the backrest (semi-reclined).
instructions: Set the machine to an appropriate weight and sit with your upper back firmly against the backrest (semi-reclined).
Position the padded roller directly over your hips and place your feet flat on the foot platform about hip-width apart, knees bent.
Begin with your hips lowered so your thighs are angled toward the platform (starting position). Grip the handles for stability.
Drive through your heels and contract your glutes to extend your hips upward until your hips are in line with your shoulders and knees (full hip extension).
Squeeze the glutes at the top for 1–2 seconds while keeping your chin tucked and spine neutral; avoid hyperextending the lower back.
Lower the lever in a controlled manner back to the starting position, allowing the hips to descend fully while maintaining tension in the glutes.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, breathing out on the concentric (hip raise) and in on the eccentric (lowering) phase.
exercises.description: Plate-loaded lever hip thrust performed from a seated/reclined position to drive the hips upward against resistance, primarily targeting the glute muscles. Feet are planted on a foot platform while a padded lever rests on the hips and the upper back is supported by the backrest.

tips: Focus on explosive concentric movements.
Take full rest between sets for neural recovery.
Prioritize form over weight - quality reps build strength.

exercises.name: Barbell Feet Flat Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set the bench inside a rack so the bar sits at arm height when you lie down; load the bar and secure collars or have a spotter.
instructions: Set the bench inside a rack so the bar sits at arm height when you lie down; load the bar and secure collars or have a spotter.
Lie supine on the flat bench with your eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and planted for stability, and a slight natural arch in the lower back.
Retract and depress your shoulder blades (pinch them together and press them into the bench) to create a stable chest platform.
Grip the bar with a full grip slightly wider than shoulder width, wrists stacked over forearms and elbows under the bar.
Unrack the bar by straightening your arms and moving the bar into the start position directly above your shoulders.
Inhale, then lower the bar in a controlled manner to mid-chest / nipple line, keeping elbows at about a 45° angle to the torso and maintaining scapular retraction.
Briefly pause with the bar lightly contacting the chest (do not bounce), then exhale and press the bar upward along a slightly arced path back to full arm extension.
Lock out the elbows at the top without overextending, keep the feet planted and core braced throughout the set.
After the final rep, carefully rack the bar onto the supports or have your spotter assist; use a spotter for heavy loads and maintain strict control on every rep.
exercises.description: Barbell feet-flat flat bench press is a supine pressing exercise that primarily works the pectorals by pressing a loaded barbell from the chest to full arm extension. Keeping the feet flat provides a stable base while the triceps and delts assist in elbow extension and shoulder stabilization.

exercises.name: Barbell Incline Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set an adjustable bench to a 30-45 degree incline, lie back with your head, upper back, and glutes firmly against the pad, and plant feet flat on the floor.
instructions: Set an adjustable bench to a 30-45 degree incline, lie back with your head, upper back, and glutes firmly against the pad, and plant feet flat on the floor.
Grip the barbell slightly wider than shoulder-width with palms facing away, unrack the bar and position it directly above your upper chest with arms extended.
Inhale and lower the bar with control to your upper chest near the collarbone, keeping elbows at a 45-60 degree angle from your torso.
Press the bar back up explosively while exhaling, extending your arms fully without locking the elbows aggressively.
Repeat for desired reps, then carefully re-rack the barbell with control.
exercises.description: The barbell incline bench press is a compound pressing movement performed on an inclined bench that emphasizes the upper portion of the pectorals while also engaging the anterior delts and triceps. It is done from a supine position on an incline bench, pressing a barbell vertically from chest to full arm extension.

exercises.name: Barbell Close Grip Larsen Press
exercises.notes: Set up on a flat bench with a barbell racked at appropriate height; lie supine with your legs extended straight out on the bench (Larsen position) rather than feet on the floor.
instructions: Set up on a flat bench with a barbell racked at appropriate height; lie supine with your legs extended straight out on the bench (Larsen position) rather than feet on the floor.
Grip the bar with a close/narrow grip (hands about shoulder-width or slightly narrower), retract your shoulder blades, and unrack the bar to hold it over your chest with arms extended.
Lower the bar under control to your chest, keeping elbows tucked closer to your body, and allow the bar to briefly rest on your chest (dead stop).
Press the bar back up by driving through your chest and triceps until arms are fully extended; maintain core tension throughout since you have no leg drive.
Repeat for desired reps, then carefully re-rack the bar.
exercises.description: A close-grip variation of the bench press performed in the Larsen style with legs extended straight on the bench rather than planted on the floor. This eliminates leg drive and increases core stability demands while the narrow grip emphasizes triceps involvement alongside the pectorals and front delts.

exercises.name: Barbell Pin Bench Press Conventional grip
exercises.notes: Set the safety pins in the power rack to a height that places the bar just above your chest when lying supine on the bench (bar will contact the pins at the bottom of the movement).
instructions: Set the safety pins in the power rack to a height that places the bar just above your chest when lying supine on the bench (bar will contact the pins at the bottom of the movement).

Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes roughly under the bar, feet flat on the floor, and a slight natural arch in the lower back; retract and depress the shoulder blades to create a stable chest platform.
Grip the bar with a conventional shoulder-width (slightly wider than shoulder) overhand grip, wrists stacked over elbows and thumbs wrapped around the bar.
Begin with the bar resting on the pins (arms either slightly bent or fully extended depending on your setup). Brace your core and take a full breath in before initiating the press.
Press the bar upward from the pins in a controlled, explosive concentric motion until the arms are fully extended and elbows locked out while exhaling.
Lower the bar under control along the same path until it gently contacts the pins at the set height (do not bounce the bar off the pins), pause as needed on the pins to reset tension.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, maintaining scapular retraction, a stable foot drive, and controlled tempo on the descent.
exercises.description: A bench press variation performed inside a rack where the bar starts and finishes on safety pins; it emphasizes the press from a dead stop to overload the pectorals through the concentric phase. Performed with a conventional (shoulder‑width) grip to target the mid-chest while the triceps and anterior delts assist.

exercises.name: Barbell Decline Pullover
exercises.notes: Set a decline bench to a moderate angle and secure your feet under the pads so your body is stable and supine on the bench.
instructions: Set a decline bench to a moderate angle and secure your feet under the pads so your body is stable and supine on the bench.
Grasp a barbell with a pronated (overhand) grip about shoulder-width apart and lift it straight over your chest with elbows slightly bent.
Brace your core and keep your hips down to maintain a neutral spine throughout the movement.
Inhale and slowly lower the barbell in a controlled arc behind your head by hinging at the shoulders; keep a slight bend in the elbows and do not let them flare excessively.
Lower until you feel a deep stretch in the chest/lats or until the bar is roughly level with the crown of your head — do not go so far that your lower back overarches.
Exhale and pull the barbell back in the same arc to the start position over your chest by contracting the pectorals and lats; keep the movement smooth and controlled.
Repeat for the desired reps, maintaining steady tempo and full control on both lowering and lifting phases.
Use a spotter or lighter weight if you cannot control the bar safely behind your head; stop if you feel sharp shoulder or back pain.
exercises.description: Lying supine on a decline bench, the barbell pullover moves the weight in an arc from over the chest to behind the head, emphasizing a deep stretch and contraction of the chest while also engaging the lats and triceps.

tips: Focus on explosive concentric movements.
Take full rest between sets for neural recovery.
Prioritize form over weight - quality reps build strength.

exercises.name: Smith Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Set the Smith machine bar at about mid-thigh height and load appropriate weight. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Set the Smith machine bar at about mid-thigh height and load appropriate weight. Stand with feet roughly shoulder-width apart, toes pointing forward.
Grip the bar with a pronated (overhand) or slightly neutral grip, hands just outside hip width. Step back so the bar pulls you slightly forward.
Hinge at the hips until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor (or a slight incline), keep a soft bend in the knees, and maintain a neutral spine (no rounding).
Brace your core and pull your shoulder blades back and down to initiate the movement.
Row the bar toward your lower ribs/upper abdomen by driving the elbows straight back, keeping elbows close to the body and squeezing the shoulder blades together at the top.
Pause briefly at the top to feel the contraction in the lats and upper back, then lower the bar under control until arms are nearly extended and tension is felt in the back.
Repeat for the desired repetitions while maintaining a strong hip hinge, neutral spine, and steady breathing (exhale during the pull, inhale during the lower).
Avoid jerking with the lower back, excessive torso rotation, or letting the hips rise — keep legs and core engaged to stabilize the movement.
exercises.description: A bent-over row performed on a Smith machine that emphasizes the lats and mid/upper back by pulling a fixed barbell toward the torso from a hip-hinged position. The legs and core stabilize the body while the arms and scapular muscles drive the pulling motion.

exercises.name: Smith Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set the Smith machine bar to an appropriate height, add desired weight, and set safety stops slightly below chest level. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Set the Smith machine bar to an appropriate height, add desired weight, and set safety stops slightly below chest level.
Position a flat bench in the machine so the bar will line up with the mid-chest when you are lying down.
Lie supine on the bench with feet flat on the floor, knees bent, and a slight natural arch in the low back; retract your shoulder blades to create a stable chest platform.
Grasp the bar with a medium grip (hands slightly wider than shoulder-width) and ensure wrists are stacked over elbows; keep head on the bench.
Unrack the bar by rotating it or disengaging the safety, then lower the bar under control to the mid-chest/sternum while keeping elbows at about a 30–60° angle from the torso.
Pause briefly with the bar lightly touching the chest (do not bounce), then press the bar up explosively until arms are nearly straight without locking the elbows aggressively.
Maintain a tight core and pressed scapulae throughout the movement for stability; avoid excessive flaring of the elbows or hyperextending the lower back.
Control the descent on each rep and re-rack the bar by rotating it back into the safety catches when finished.
exercises.description: A Smith machine variation of the flat bench press performed lying supine on a flat bench, pressing the fixed bar vertically to work the chest. The guided bar path reduces stabilization demand while emphasizing pressing strength in the pectorals.

exercises.name: Lever Lateral Raise with Chest Support
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat so your chest rests firmly against the pad and your feet are flat on the floor; the lever handles should be at about hip height when your arms hang down. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Adjust the seat so your chest rests firmly against the pad and your feet are flat on the floor; the lever handles should be at about hip height when your arms hang down.
Sit with your chest pressed into the pad, grip the handles with a neutral or overhand grip, and keep a small bend in the elbows throughout the movement.
Start with the arms hanging at your sides (shoulders relaxed) and brace your core to prevent movement of the torso.
Exhale and raise both arms out to the sides in a controlled arc until your upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor (about 90 degrees), keeping the elbows slightly bent and the wrists neutral.
Pause briefly at the top while avoiding shoulder shrugging (do not lift with the neck or upper trapezius).
Inhale and slowly lower the handles back to the start position under control, maintaining chest contact with the pad and minimizing momentum.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, choosing a weight that allows strict form without swinging.
exercises.description: Seated chest-supported lateral raise performed on a lever machine to isolate the lateral deltoids; the chest pad prevents torso swing so the delts do the work. Arms are raised out to the sides with a slight elbow bend and controlled tempo.

tips: Every working set should reach true muscular failure.
Use slow, controlled tempo throughout the movement.
Allow full recovery between exercises - this is not cardio.
